title: Physical Standards and Medical Requirements for Indian Armed Forces
author: DefenceRoger Editorial
data: Physical and medical fitness are absolute prerequisites to join the Indian Military. Aspiring candidates must meet specific criteria for height, weight, and chest expansion. Visual standards (such as 6/6 vision, refraction limits) are strictly checked, especially for the Air Force Flying Branch. Common temporary and permanent rejection grounds include flat feet, knock knees, cubitus valgus, DNS, and dental points below 14. Regular exercise, stamina building, and posture correction can resolve minor issues.
